Footsquare - Overview - 1: Footsquare is a team sport played on a rounded pitch with raised baskets instead of goals. The game emphasizes continuous play, precise passing, and 360-degree spatial awareness.
Footsquare - Overview - 2: Two teams compete to score by placing the ball into the opponent's basket. The team with the higher score at full time wins.
Footsquare - The Pitch - 1: The pitch is circular with a clearly marked boundary line. The boundary line is part of the field of play.
Footsquare - The Pitch - 2: A central circle with a radius of 6 m marks the kickoff area. A surrounding ring (the control ring) extends from 6 m to 12 m.
Footsquare - The Pitch - 3: Four quadrant lines divide the pitch into equal arcs. These lines are used for restarts and zone-based rules.
Footsquare - The Pitch - 4: The surface may be natural or artificial. Artificial surfaces must be non-reflective and uniform in color.
Footsquare - Baskets - 1: Each team defends a raised basket positioned at opposite ends of a diameter line. Baskets are circular and mounted 2.5 m above the ground.
Footsquare - Baskets - 2: The basket has a rim diameter of 0.9 m with a shallow net. A score counts only when the ball fully passes through the rim from above.
Footsquare - Baskets - 3: A 5 m scoring arc is marked around each basket. Shots taken from outside the arc are worth 2 points; inside the arc are worth 1 point.
Footsquare - The Ball - 1: The ball is size 5 with a textured surface for grip. Only one ball is used unless the referee authorizes a replacement.
Footsquare - The Ball - 2: If the ball becomes defective, play is stopped and restarted with a dropped ball at the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Players - 1: Each team has 7 players on the field, including one keeper. A match cannot start or continue with fewer than 5 players.
Footsquare - Players - 2: Substitutions are rolling. A team may substitute at any stoppage and after a goal is scored.
Footsquare - Players - 3: The keeper may handle the ball only within the keeper zone, a 4 m radius circle beneath the basket.
Footsquare - Equipment - 1: Basic equipment: shirt, shorts, socks, footwear, and shinguards. Jewelry is not permitted.
Footsquare - Equipment - 2: Teams must wear colors that distinguish them from opponents and match officials.
Footsquare - Match Duration - 1: The match consists of three 12-minute periods with 3 minutes of rest between periods.
Footsquare - Match Duration - 2: Time is stopped for injuries, disciplinary sanctions, and video reviews.
Footsquare - Start and Restarts - 1: The match begins with a center kick from the central circle. Opponents must remain outside the control ring.
Footsquare - Start and Restarts - 2: After a score, the team that conceded restarts with a center kick.
Footsquare - Start and Restarts - 3: If the ball crosses the boundary line, play restarts with a throw-in from the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Start and Restarts - 4: A dropped ball is used for neutral stoppages and is taken at the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Scoring - 1: A score is valid when the ball fully passes through the basket rim from above without illegal contact.
Footsquare - Scoring - 2: Goals from outside the 5 m scoring arc are worth 2 points; inside the arc are worth 1 point.
Footsquare - Scoring - 3: If a defending player touches the basket or rim during a shot, an automatic 2-point score is awarded.
Footsquare - Movement and Contact - 1: Physical contact is limited. Shoulder-to-shoulder contact is allowed if both players are playing the ball.
Footsquare - Movement and Contact - 2: Sliding tackles are not permitted. Any slide toward an opponent is a foul.
Footsquare - Fouls - 1: A foul results in a free shot from the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Fouls - 2: Handball: deliberate handling by a non-keeper is a foul. Accidental handball may be penalized if it creates an advantage.
Footsquare - Fouls - 3: Persistent infringement results in a yellow card.
Footsquare - Cards and Sanctions - 1: Yellow card: formal warning. Two yellow cards in the same match result in a red card.
Footsquare - Cards and Sanctions - 2: Red card: the player must leave the field and their team plays short for 5 minutes (game clock).
Footsquare - Cards and Sanctions - 3: After the 5-minute penalty, the team may replace the sent-off player.
Footsquare - Advantage - 1: The referee may apply advantage if stopping play would benefit the offending team.
Footsquare - Keeper Zone - 1: Only the keeper may enter the keeper zone with the ball. Field players may enter without the ball but cannot obstruct the keeper.
Footsquare - Keeper Zone - 2: If a keeper holds the ball for more than 6 seconds, a free shot is awarded to the opponents.
Footsquare - Offside Equivalent - 1: Footsquare uses a zone rule instead of offside. Attacking players may not receive the ball inside the opponent's keeper zone.
Footsquare - Offside Equivalent - 2: Receiving the ball inside the keeper zone results in a turnover at the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Referee - 1: The referee enforces the rules, manages time, and may use video review for scoring decisions.
Footsquare - Referee - 2: The referee's decisions are final, except for the use of video review on scoring plays.
Footsquare - Video Review - 1: Video review may be used to confirm scores and basket interference.
Footsquare - Video Review - 2: A review must be initiated within 30 seconds of the event.
Footsquare - Tie Break - 1: If the score is tied after three periods, a 5-minute golden-point period is played.
Footsquare - Tie Break - 2: The first team to score in golden-point wins the match.
Footsquare - Conduct - 1: Dissent, abusive language, and unsporting conduct are sanctionable offences.
Footsquare - Conduct - 2: Deliberate time-wasting results in a free shot and a yellow card.
Footsquare - Restarts Summary - 1: Throw-in: taken from the nearest quadrant line when the ball leaves the field.
Footsquare - Restarts Summary - 2: Free shot: awarded for fouls, taken from the nearest quadrant line.
Footsquare - Restarts Summary - 3: Center kick: starts each period and follows every score.
